title: System for bath shower and methods employed thereof
abstract: Exemplary embodiments of the present disclosure are directed towards a system for bath shower comprising of: a water heating unit for heating the cold water flowing from a water inlet and pressurising the water with a controlled pump to the water heating unit which is tank less and is operated through an electric heater coil; an electrical control unit comprising of a printed circuit board and associated electronic components to mechanically support and electronically connect a plurality of electrical components which are configured to display and control a plurality of electrical parameters; and a shower unit comprising of a shower head with a plurality of nozzles which have multiple effects not limited to umbrella effect, cascading effect, and heavy fall droplet effect to deliver water from the shower head to effectively and substantially reduce the quantity of the water required for a normal bath and also reduce the power required for heating the water, thus making the system an environment friendly green initiative to optimise the use of natural resources and non-renewable energy
claims:
Claims CLAIMS  What is claimed is: 1. A system for bath shower comprising of: a water heating unit for heating the cold water flowing from a water inlet, whereby the water heating unit is tankless and configured to be operated through an electric heater coil ; an electrical unit comprising of a printed circuit board to mechanically support and electronically connect a plurality of electrical components, whereby the plurality of components are configured to display and control a plurality of electrical parameters; and a shower unit comprising of a shower head with a plurality of nozzles, whereby the plurality of nozzles are configured to have at least one of: umbrella effect; cascading effect; and heavy rain fall droplet effect to deliver water from the shower head. 2. The system of claim 1, wherein the nozzles are configured with an orifice size varying between 50 microns to 2000 microns and/or 100 microns to 3000 microns. 3. The system of claim 1, wherein a filtering unitseparates heavy particles in water by gravity separation thereby eliminates formation of clogging due to hard water. 4. The system of claim 1, wherein one nozzle could also be a flat nozzle with an orifice size varying between 50 microns to 2000 microns. 5. The system of claim 1 , wherein the electrical unit is isolated from the tankless water heating unit and is configured to be not in contact with the water running in the pipe. 6. The system of claim 1 , wherein the sensors are configured to sense adequate pressure and temperature. 7. The system of claim 1 , wherein the building of pressure is through at least one of: a centrifugal; pneumatic; and diaphragm AC and DC pumps. 8. The system of claim 1, wherein the water delivery has at least one of: a cascading jet effect; an umberella effect; and a heavy rain fall drop effect. 9. The method of working of tankless water heater and shower system comprising of: establishing the connectivity between a rigid inlet water pipe with an electrical unit, whereby the cold water flows from the inlet water pipe to a tankless water heater for an output of hot water; filtering of the flowing water and separation of heavy particles through a transparent mesh, whereby the filtration occurs for a subsequent time through a secondary stainless steel mesh, optionally if required, and removes solid particles from the water flowing through the pipe; starting the AC and DC pumps and the heater upon the passing of water by the flow sensors and temperature sensors, whereby the flow sensors detect the stoppage in the flow of water and the temperature sensors detect the increase in temperatures unconditionally and signal through LED status display and the unit stops automatically under no water flow conditions and under overheating of the water beyond the set temperature as required; and 

 diverting the flow of heated water through a flow diverter, whereby the diversions are into at least one of: a mass water collection unit; and a hand held shower to obtain the desired effect. 10. The method of claim 8, wherein the nozzles are non-clogging and are configured to be used as a single nozzle and as a combination of nozzles to deliver the water in droplet sizes between 450 microns to 3000 microns.
